---
title: "Reference"
subtitle: "Quick lookup for skills, layers, and the Dittonomics migration map"
---

# Quick Lookup

## Core workflow skills

| Upstream intent | Dittonomics entry point |
|---|---|
| new project pipeline | `$clo-new-project` |
| discovery | `$clo-discover` |
| identification strategy | `$clo-strategize` |
| analysis and outputs | `$clo-analyze` |
| drafting | `$clo-write` |
| review and peer review | `$clo-review` |
| revision cycle | `$clo-revise` |
| talks | `$clo-talk` |
| submission package | `$clo-submit` |
| maintenance and upgrade | `$clo-research-tools` |

## Install locations

| Item | Generic location |
|---|---|
| public starter bundle | `.codex/` in this repo |
| user skills | `~/.codex/skills` |
| custom subagents | `~/.codex/agents` |
| repo-local skills | `.agents/skills/` |
| session constitution | `AGENTS.md` |
| repo overlay | `AGENTS.override.md` |
| optional voice layer | `~/voice` |

# Workflow Kernel Files

The public Dittonomics starter now treats these files as the core kernel references:

- `pipeline-contract.md`
- `artifact-contracts.md`
- `agent-registry.md`
- `routing-rules.md`

# Voice Setup Files

The public voice starter uses this recommended structure:

- `core_voice.md`
- `voice_examples.md`
- `econ_paper_register.md`
- `style_hierarchy.md`
- `collaboration_register.md` optional
- `journal_tightening_register.md` optional

Voice is optional. The public templates document the structure without exposing one user's private prose system.

# Dittonomics Migration Cheatsheet

| If Clo-Author says | In Dittonomics use | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `CLAUDE.md` | `AGENTS.md` plus `AGENTS.override.md` | Keep the constitution lean and move detail outward |
| `.claude/rules` | active workflow references plus repo overlays | Use source mirrors only when provenance matters |
| `.claude/references` | active `clo-workflow` references | Domain, journal, exploration, migration, and kernel guidance |
| slash commands | explicit `$clo-*` skills | Preserve names, change invocation style |
| `.claude/agents` | `~/.codex/agents` custom subagents | Main Codex session remains orchestrator |
| distributable `.claude/` bundle in the repo | forkable `.codex/` starter folder in the repo | Public starter kit for AGENTS, skills, agents, config, and quick reference |
| settings and hidden behaviors | `config.toml`, `AGENTS.md`, and explicit utilities | Avoid undocumented hidden behavior |
| implicit writing style | optional `~/voice` layer | Keep prose rules explicit and modular |
| `.claude` exploration instructions | `explorations/` folder plus exploration reference | Create the folder when missing |
| orchestrator agent | main Codex session | No separate orchestrator install needed |

# Worker-Critic Map

| Creator | Reviewer |
|---|---|
| `librarian` | `librarian_critic` |
| `explorer` | `explorer_critic` |
| `strategist` | `strategist_critic` |
| `coder` | `coder_critic` |
| `writer` | `writer_critic` |
| `storyteller` | `storyteller_critic` |

# Persistence Rule

A core Dittonomics rule is now explicit:

- skills resolve paths first
- creator agents write only to named targets
- critics, referees, editor, and verifier return reports to the parent orchestrator
- the parent orchestrator owns report saving, renaming, and promotion unless it explicitly delegates a write target

# Exploration Workflow Mapping

| Workflow need | Dittonomics rule |
|---|---|
| prototype estimation idea | create or use `explorations/[name]/` |
| temporary side output | keep it inside the exploration until promoted |
| abandoned branch | archive under `explorations/ARCHIVE/` |
| no exploration folder yet | create `explorations/README.md` and `explorations/ARCHIVE/` first |

# Attribution and Scope

This reference is intentionally compact. It is here to make the upstream Clo-Author manual readable to a Codex user and to surface the key Dittonomics contracts without forcing readers through the entire starter bundle.
